fields.description:
## Why
Production-severity axes + the mean structurally mis-measure code-review's maintainability/latent-regression
findings: a feature silently dead in prod (untested wiring) scores 0.415 because prod_impact is 'none' and
reversibility 'easy' drag it down. Landmines and nits overlap so no scalar threshold separates them.

## What
A code-review-specific, verifier-embedded impact model dispatched per-gate off `decide.py` (see the
kernel-isolation section). Concrete definitions:
- TIER VALUES: each consequence binary carries the severity it implies if true — minor=0.3, moderate=0.6,
  serious=0.9. Lane-severity = MAX of the tiers of the TRUE binaries (no dilution; a minor/moderate binary
  cannot alone reach the block zone). No cross-binary compounding (conservative default).
- TWO LANES: prod-lane = MAX(correctness tier-tagged binaries) x likelihood-of-firing (common=1.0 /
  sometimes=0.6 / rare=0.25); maint-lane = MAX(maintainability tier-tagged binaries) x change-frequency
  floor (see below). `impact_base = MAX(prod-lane, maint-lane)`.
- REVERSIBILITY FLOOR (det/hybrid): if the change touches a hard-to-reverse surface (migration dir, exported
  public API, persisted-data/serialization format, deletion, released artifact), floor a borderline result
  up: `impact = max(impact_base, 0.6)`; easy-to-reverse adds no floor.
- CHANGE-FREQUENCY FLOOR (maint-lane): git-log churn count of the touched file over the last 90 days,
  normalized to [0.5,1.0] as a multiplier (hotspot amplification); det from `git log`.
- DETECTION AMPLIFIER: silent-failure OR escapes-automation -> x1.0; else x0.8.
- RE-ANCHOR: drop the 0.33 attribute floor; co-calibrate the tier values + block threshold on the
  code-review labeled/adjudicated set so a maintainability landmine reaches ~0.8+ and a nit ~0.15-0.2.
- SUB-ANSWER SCHEMA: the tier-tagged binary sub-answers are emitted as new fields inside the SINGLE
  aggregate Pass-2 verify structured output (alongside the existing severity_attributes) — no added LLM
  calls. Reversibility/reach/change-frequency are DET/hybrid (computed from the diff + git), not LLM.
- CARVED SUB-QUESTION SET (audit-approved, each serious binary carries its charitable carve-out): serious =
  {data-loss-without-recovery, security-bypass-not-enforced-elsewhere, silent-wrong-feeding-a-decision,
  unversioned-published-contract-break, safety-net-removal-without-replacement}; plus the moderate/minor
  coverage set (capability-degraded, drift, hidden-invariant, implicit-coupling, dead-code).

## Kernel isolation (shared decide.py)
`decide.impact()` is called by BOTH gates. This child adds a code-review impact function selected by the
injected gate identity (mirroring `threshold_for(gate=...)`); the plan-review branch is byte-unchanged and a
test asserts plan-review verdicts are unaffected. This novel two-lane model is an architectural decision:
capture it in an ADR under `docs/adr/`.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` (code-review `impact()` + per-gate dispatch), the code-review
  verify prompt/schema (emit the carved tier-tagged sub-answers), the DET signals (reversibility/reach/
  change-frequency), the re-anchoring calibration, and the ADR. Plan-review impact unchanged.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Code-review `impact = MAX(prod-lane, maint-lane)` with tier-tagged-binary MAX within each lane using
      the stated tier values; reversibility floor + change-frequency floor + detection amplifier + re-anchored
      scale applied per the formulas above.
- [ ] The carved serious/moderate/minor sub-question set is implemented with charitable carve-outs.
- [ ] Sub-answers emitted in ONE aggregate Pass-2 call (no added LLM calls); reversibility/reach/change-
      frequency are DET/hybrid; per-gate dispatch leaves plan-review byte-unchanged (asserted by test).
- [ ] An ADR records the two-lane model decision.
- [ ] Simulation on the labeled/adjudicated set shows the model separates HIGH from NIT better than the old
      scalar (or the gap is explained), validated diff-grounded before any threshold-down tuning.
